summary: Decompiled a substantial set of Samus missile item functions, added Samus missile item vars/attributes and declarations, moved the missile spawn prototype to the missile header, and tightened several item/fighter GObj types needed by the missile code. The decomp.dev bot reported 14 newly matched functions in itsamusmissile and 5 additional partial improvements, raising matched code by +2276 bytes. The author noted they tried the last three functions but left them because they were too difficult for now.
what_changed: Added about 411 lines of new C implementation in src/melee/it/items/itsamusmissile.c for Samus missile spawn, lifetime/update, normal and smash missile initialization, animation/physics callbacks, collision reactions, reflected behavior, state transitions, and owner cleanup.

Implemented it_802B62D0 as the Samus missile spawn function returning Item_GObj*, constructing SpawnItem with It_Kind_Samus_Missile, setting parent fields, smash-missile flag, owner tracking, and choosing it_802B66A8 or it_802B6A60 based on missile type.
Added itSamusMissileAttributes and itSamusMissile_ItemVars in itCharItems.h, then added samusmissile to the Item xDD4 itemVar union in it/types.h.

Expanded itsamusmissile.h with prototypes for the newly decompiled missile functions, m2c stack-shape structs for partially matched functions, and moved the it_802B62D0 declaration out of itsamuschargeshot.h.

Changed Samus side-special missile creation in ftSs_SpecialN.c to include itsamusmissile.h, use GET_FIGHTER and ftCheckThrowB0, use FtPart_56 instead of raw parts[56], compare against ftSs_MS_SpecialS and ftSs_MS_SpecialAirS instead of numeric motion IDs, and pass bool-style smash missile arguments to it_802B62D0.
Added FtPart_56 = 56 to Fighter_Part in ft/forward.h for the missile spawn joint lookup.
Retyped ftLib_80086368 from HSD_GObj* to Fighter_GObj* for both return value and fighter argument, reflecting that it returns a fighter object.
Replaced the previous disabled/asm/NOT_IMPLEMENTED version of it_8026B634 with a C implementation that passes the missing facing_dir float to ftLib_80086368 and returns Fighter_GObj*.
Retyped Item_80268B18 from HSD_GObj* to Item_GObj*, matching its airborne item spawn role and allowing missile spawn code to use item-specific GObj typing.
Factored the repeated 'grandchild JObj' lookup into itGetJObjGrandchild in it/inlines.h and reused it in itthunder.c and missile effect spawning.

The decomp.dev report showed 14 new 100% matches: it_2725_Logic52_Reflected, it_802B62D0, it_802B63F8, it_802B66A8, it_802B6A60, itSamusmissile_UnkMotion0_Anim, itSamusmissile_UnkMotion1_Anim, itSamusmissile_UnkMotion1_Phys, it_802B701C, it_802B70A0, it_2725_Logic52_DmgDealt, it_2725_Logic52_Clanked, it_2725_Logic52_HitShield, and it_2725_Logic52_EvtUnk.
The same report listed partial improvements for itSamusmissile_UnkMotion0_Phys at 97.20%, it_802B64FC at 88.55%, it_2725_Logic52_ShieldBounced at 95.61%, plus .sdata and .sdata2 improvements.
smart_moves: Solving the missing ftLib_80086368 argument let it_8026B634 be converted from hand-written assembly/placeholder code into normal C and also made missile homing/target lookup calls type-check cleanly.

Using typed aliases like Fighter_GObj* and Item_GObj* reduced ambiguous HSD_GObj usage in APIs that are fighter- or item-specific, which helped the Samus missile code express ownership and spawn behavior more accurately.
Introducing itGetJObjGrandchild avoided duplicating a null-safe JObj child->child traversal and immediately reused an identical pattern from itThunder.
Replacing raw numbers with FtPart_56 and ftSs_MS_SpecialS/ftSs_MS_SpecialAirS made the Samus missile spawn code more maintainable without changing the low-level behavior.

Keeping m2c-style constructs where needed, including PAD_STACK, goto block labels, M2C_FIELD uses, and stack-layout structs, preserved matching progress for difficult functions rather than forcing premature cleanup.
Moving the missile spawn declaration from the charge shot header to itsamusmissile.h clarified ownership of it_802B62D0 and reduced misleading cross-item coupling.
decomp_lessons: When an item helper appears impossible because a called function has the wrong arity, audit the callee prototype: it_8026B634 matched once ftLib_80086368 accepted the facing_dir float.
Use item/fighter-specific GObj typedefs in signatures when the function semantically requires them; doing so can unlock downstream matches and makes owner/spawn code less error-prone.
Shared tiny inlines are valuable when multiple item files need the same exact null-check sequence; factoring them can improve readability while preserving codegen if the helper remains inline.

For projectile articles, model xDD4 item variables and xC4 article special attributes early. The Samus missile implementation relies on named item vars for smash-vs-normal state, owner, timers, angle, and effect/state transitions.
Partial decomp is acceptable and searchable when clearly left with evidence such as m2c stack structs, M2C_FIELD accesses, and author notes about remaining hard functions.
Prefer motion-state enum names and Fighter_Part enum constants over raw numeric IDs once the values are known; it documents behavior and reduces future rework.
naming_conventions: Item variable structs followed the project pattern itSamusMissile_ItemVars, with fields exposed through ip->xDD4_itemVar.samusmissile.
Article attribute structs followed itSamusMissileAttributes and retained offset-style field names x0, x4, x8, xC, etc. because semantic names were not yet proven.
Missile callbacks retained address/logic names such as it_802B62D0, it_802B63F8, it_2725_Logic52_DmgDealt, and itSamusmissile_UnkMotion0_Phys where better names were not established.
The new inline helper was named itGetJObjGrandchild but explicitly carried a @todo saying it should probably be renamed because its real semantic meaning is still uncertain.
Typed GObj aliases use Fighter_GObj* for fighter objects and Item_GObj* for item objects instead of generic HSD_GObj* where evidence supports the narrower type.
Known enum replacements included FtPart_56 for fp->parts[56] and ftSs_MS_SpecialS / ftSs_MS_SpecialAirS for raw motion IDs 349 and 351.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed a MWERKS_GEKKO inline assembly block for it_8026B634 by correcting its signature to Fighter_GObj* it_8026B634(Vec3*, Vec3*, Item_GObj*, float) and passing the float through to ftLib_80086368.
Used PAD_STACK in several functions, including the Samus side-special missile call site and missile init/reflection/state-transition functions, to preserve stack layout for matching.
Kept m2c-derived control flow in difficult functions such as it_802B64FC, including explicit labels like block_4/block_18 and M2C_FIELD accesses for offsets x18/x1C.
Added m2c stack structs in itsamusmissile.h for it_802B64FC and itSamusmissile_UnkMotion0_Phys, documenting stack Vec3 placement needed for ongoing matching.
Used bool literals at call sites but kept the it_802B62D0 implementation parameter as int in the diff excerpt, indicating codegen/signature matching still constrained the exact type.
Used math/trig helpers such as atan2f, M_PI_2, deg_to_rad, vector normalization, angle, subtraction, and rotation in missile homing/reflection physics.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The only issue comment was the decomp.dev bot report: matched code increased to 53.28% with +0.06% and +2276 bytes, 14 new matches, and 5 partial improvements.
The author's PR body explicitly says they tried the last three functions too, but those were out of scope for now; this is evidence that some missile functions remained difficult/unmatched.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish the remaining Samus missile functions mentioned as too difficult in PR #2183.
Search for later renames or semantic field names for itSamusMissileAttributes x0-x3C and itSamusMissile_ItemVars x4/x8/x10.
Check whether itGetJObjGrandchild was later renamed or replaced with a more semantic item joint helper.
Look for future changes to it_802B64FC, itSamusmissile_UnkMotion0_Phys, and it_2725_Logic52_ShieldBounced to see how the partial m2c-style code was cleaned up.
Search for other PRs that retyped HSD_GObj* APIs to Fighter_GObj* or Item_GObj* to unlock matches.
